last_item: Band C: Toast → sonner. Deleted the hand-rolled toast system (src/components/Toast.tsx: ToastProvider context + useToast() rendering a bottom-right .toast-stack with manual × + hand-timed dismiss) and replaced with shadcn sonner. Mounted <Toaster/> (src/components/ui/sonner.tsx) once at App root in App.tsx, sibling of <Routes> inside SessionProvider so it covers /login + the shell. Call sites already used toast.success/error/info(msg) — exactly sonner's toast API — so the ~15 callers across 7 files (Backups ×2, Layout, Pooler ×2, RolesDatabases ×2, Migrate ×4, Alerts ×3, Settings) just swapped `import {useToast} from @/components/Toast`+`const toast=useToast()` for `import {toast} from sonner`; firing calls byte-for-byte unchanged. Simplified generated sonner.tsx: stripped next-themes useTheme() (no ThemeProvider in this Vite SPA — it themes via prefers-color-scheme @media, so theme="system" follows OS the same way) and added closeButton (manual-dismiss parity for the old ×). Deleted dead .toast* CSS + toast-only @keyframes slide. Tests: Layout.test/Pooler.test dropped ToastProvider wrappers (sonner toast is module-level, no provider needed); Migrate.test swapped its @/components/Toast mock for a sonner mock.
last_result: green: typecheck, 95 web tests, build, go build (outside sandbox; snap-confine cap_dac_override blocks go in-sandbox). ui-heuristics-reviewer: addressed the blocking finding (next-themes useTheme with no mounted ThemeProvider → stripped it, theme="system") and the parity gap (lost × affordance → added closeButton). Accepted-as-OK: Toaster placement at App root; sonner's built-in aria-live (improvement over old). Error toast lifetime now sonner-default 4s (was 7s) — accepted: hover-paused + always manually dismissable. next-themes now an orphan dep in package.json — deferred pruning to band E (lockfile churn needs network). Next in band C: none left — band C primitives complete; advance to band D (views), starting with Login. Deferred to band E: --info-soft collides with --primary-soft; prune next-themes dep.
notes: PURE UI/UX LOOP. The prior 57-iteration hardening track is COMPLETE and shipped as v0.6.0. This track rebuilds the web panel on shadcn/ui — see PROMPT.md + UI-RULES.md + backlog.md. The operator EXPLICITLY authorized the shadcn/Tailwind/Radix dependency tree, overriding the earlier rule-4/5 objection — do NOT re-litigate whether to adopt shadcn; it is required. Start at band A (scaffold), then B (shell), C (primitives), D (views one at a time), E (cleanup). Never touch Go/business logic — frontend only.
north_star: Every view in the indiepg panel rebuilt with shadcn/ui — clean, simple, consistent — with no hand-rolled component where a shadcn one exists, behavior identical, all tests green.
